title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (May 20)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: A Colonial home in Andover sold for $2,070,000; it was built in 1986 on a 57,717-square-foot lot.
A two-family building in Arlington, built in 1872, sold for $2,580,000 with 3,035 square feet and 6 bedrooms.
A one-family Colonial in Bellingham, built in 2021, was purchased for $1,000,000 on a 48,787-square-foot lot.

title: MBTA announces new ferry service starting in June that will loop around Boston Harbor
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: MBTA to launch 'Harbor Loop' ferry service on June 29, connecting Lovejoy Wharf, Central Wharf, Seaport, and Logan Airport.
Harbor Loop will run weekdays, every 30 minutes during peak hours, with morning and evening operations but no midday service.
A one-way fare is $2.40 with tap to pay, cash, or credit card; CharlieCards not accepted on the service.

title: ‘The world responded’: More than a thousand people attend funeral for East Bridgewater veteran who left behind no family
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Bernard Arnold III, a 98-year-old WWII veteran, died May 6 with no known family in East Bridgewater.
More than 1,500 people attended Arnold's funeral, influenced by a viral Facebook post urging a community tribute.
Arnold was honored at St. Joseph the Worker Catholic Church and laid to rest in Cedar Knoll Cemetery, Taunton.

title: Revisiting our bold preseason predictions for 2025-26 Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins earned 100 points and a playoff spot under new coach Marco Sturm in the 2025-26 season.
Jeremy Swayman rebounded as the Bruins' goalie, earning a Vezina Trophy finalist nod with improved performance metrics.
Fraser Minten won the 7th Player Award for the Bruins, excelling as a dependable two-way player in his inaugural season.

title: 4 Rhode Island firefighters reinstated, 1 facing charges, after investigation into drug allegations
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Four Pawtucket firefighters reinstated after internal investigation into drug allegations, with one facing criminal charges.
Terrence O’Neill, a former firefighter, was criminally indicted for delivery of controlled substances after resigning.
Guglietta and Sunquist awaiting arbitration hearings to challenge their terminations related to the drug allegations.

title: ‘You’d have to ask the voters’: Bill Belichick reacts to Pro Football Hall of Fame snub
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bill Belichick did not receive enough votes for Pro Football Hall of Fame in his first eligible year.
Belichick emphasized his focus on coaching at the University of North Carolina amid the Hall of Fame snub.
Reactions to Belichick's omission came from sports figures like Patrick Mahomes and Tom Brady, expressing surprise and disagreement.

title: Person snuck into Harvard dorm, attacked student from behind, school says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: An unknown male entered Harvard's Lowell House and allegedly assaulted a student Tuesday evening.
The suspect attempted to force the student into their room but fled after the victim screamed.
Authorities are actively investigating the incident and searching for the suspect.

title: Ken Rosenthal explains why Red Sox don’t plan to start selling at this stage of 2026 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox are not planning to trade players despite a 20-27 record and struggles in the 2026 season.
Ken Rosenthal cites the weak American League as a reason the Red Sox won't sell yet, being close to a Wild Card spot.
Injuries and poor offensive performance have hindered the team, currently ranked 29th in MLB in runs per game.

title: Boston broke the heat record at 96 degrees. Here’s how long the steamy weather will last.
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: Boston set a new record high temperature of 96 degrees on May 19, surpassing previous records for the date.
The National Weather Service forecasts cooler weather to begin on Thursday, following near 90-degree temperatures on Wednesday.
This heat marks the second hottest spring day on record for Boston and Springfield, tying Manchester, NH for the top spot.

title: 3 stabbed, 2 arrested in chaotic scene at R.I. town beach, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Three people were stabbed at Narragansett Town Beach, Rhode Island; all had minor injuries, no suspects identified.
Two individuals were arrested for disorderly conduct and related charges during unrelated disturbances at the same location.
Rhode Island State Police and other agencies responded; police seek information from the public about the incidents.
